series_description: Includes published reports, unpublished substan- tive reports and policy studies.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Aduitor of State and audit report is released. Destroy if superseded and of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Copies of state and federal legislation of interest to an agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years or until audited by the Auditor of State and audit report is released

series_description: A form that is used to deposit, retrieve, return, and destroy records at the State Records Center.
retention_statement: Paper: For deposits, destructs, and returns, retain five years after transaction is completed

series_description: A form used to identify the length of time a record must be retained before final disposition.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded by a revised retention schedule, or until record series in no longer created

series_description: Manuals produced by an agency for internal per- sonnel training.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of adminis- trative value

series_description: Executive Budget Proposal: Table 2-Federal Grants and State Match Detail; Table 4-Continuation, Required or Expanded Proposal; Table 4s-Continuation (C), required (R), Expanded (E); Table 5-Equipment Schedule; Table C-1 Capital Project Proposal.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year after the end of biennium

series_description: Reserves funds and formally recognizes the State's obligation to pay for miscellaneous goods and/or services that require no other formal approval. Used so funds will not lapse.
Deed records, real property.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain permanently. Upon disposal of the property, this instrument and all related papers are to remain with the record copy of the state conveyance.

series_description: Payroll Warrant Cancellation Advise & Transmittal. Used to cancel a payroll warrant issued by an agency in error.
Forms used by a person/organization to enter into contract with an agency to request payment for services rendered or to amend contract. May include attendance forms, correspondence, etc.
Used to submit specifications for a printing job to State Printing and to encumber the dollars needed for the job.
Used for the salvage of equipment or property of the state of Ohio.
Request for stop/release of warrant.

retention_statement: Paper: Retain in Board office for a period of 2 fiscal years

series_description: Accounts payable ledger.
To request Controlling Board approval of emergency expenditures for Capital Improvement Projects, purchases made without bidwaiver, and transfer of Capital Improvement Funds.
Used as a management tool for one-time expenses not covered by DAS Directives.
Authorizes payment to another state agency for goods and/or services received and services as the basis for disbursing funds.
(Accounts Payable Invoices) Bill for goods shipped or servcies rendered. They are matched with purchase orders and rec- eiving reports, and attached to vouchers for payment.
Agencies use this form to summarize all out-of- state travel done by personnel for each quarter.

series_title: GA-19 Newspaper Realeses and News Bullet
series_description: Includes new/press releases and bulletins issued by an agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in agency until no longer of administrative value

series_description: Listing of long distance calls made by agency personnel for a particular time period.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released

series_description: Worksheet used for proposed budgets.
Periodic financial statement.

Correspondence of the head of an agency (Director, Chief, Secretary, Commissioner, and their deputies), dealing with all aspects of the administration of their offices. Correspondence includes information concerning agency policies, procedures, program,
retention_statement: Paper: Retain four years. Review by State Archives for possible transfer. Destroy if of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: All records which document or are the basis for a payroll including time reports, activity sheets, time cards, overtime, com- pensation time, flextime authorizations, and leave requests.
Capital Asset Records
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years after asset is sold

series_description: Used to record receipts or repayment where the original payment was in error, duplicated or made illegally.
Expenditures documents. Authorize payment to a vendor other than a state agency for goods and/or services received.
Claims and Litigation Records
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after case is closed

series_description: Official minutes, orders and directives of a state agency, commission, board, or council.
Used to establish descriptive information for each capital project and to update previously entered capital projects data.
Represents an agency's intent to purchase goods and/or services; it reserves appropriation funds for future spending until the funds are formally obligated (encumbered).

Records inventory and analysis worksheet: Used for conducting an inventory of an agency's record series. Requests vital information to be used in determining the retention period and disposition of a record.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded by an up- dated inventory or until record series is no longer created

series_description: Used to certify the destruction or transfer of records according to an approved records retention schedule.
Requests for brochures, maps, and other free publications provided to the public by an agency which are answered by stand- ard form letters.
Reports created by state agency staff or outside consultants concerning the management or operations of a state agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years. Reivew by State Archives for possible transfer. Destroy if of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Includes negatives, prints, slides, transparencies and related items.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain untiled information is no longer current. Review every two years by State Archives for possible transfer. Destroy if of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Used to refer complaints concerning vendor's unsatisfactory products, services, and performance.
Used by State Accounting to indicate errors on documents returned to an agency site.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until error is corrected

series_description: Original computer printout, instructions and work sheets used to complete equipment inventory reports.
Used by OBM to define allocation units and by agencies to define their Spending Authority Codes (SACs).
Internal form used to obtain authorization for out-of-state travel for a state employee.

An order for goods and services by a state agency from a specified outside vendor or seller (other than another state agency). It represents an obligation to pay for goods or services and formally encum- bers the funds to do so.

retention_statement: Paper: Retain in Board office for 5 years (fiscal)

series_description: Also includes other published periodic reports on agency activities.
retention_statement: Paper: Upon publication, fulfill the re- quirements of O.R.C. 149.11

series_description: Executive Orders, Proclamations, and Legislative Advisories: Instructions issued by the Governor as the Chief Executive of State Government. (Original copy at Governor's office transferred to State Archives after five years.)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_description: Used for input in the statewide computer system and to gain approval of all state forms from State Forms Center at DAS Information Management.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years after form is obsolete

series_description: Includes mailing lists, directories, rosters, and registers compiled by an agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_title: GA-17 Minutes of Agency Staff Meetings
series_description: Minutes and supporting records documenting internal policy decisions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years. Review by State Archives for possible transfer. Destroy if of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Printed or recorded copies of public speeches given by representatives of state agencies.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of adminis- trative value to agencies. State Archives will review for possible transfer. Destroy if of no historical interest to State Archives.

series_description: List of all positions within an agency by position control number, including both filled and vacant positions. Used for budget and personnel purposes.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded by a new roster

series_description: Computer printout in two parts listing all employees by agency, employee name, social security number and other pertinent payroll information. White copy is corrected and returned to DAS/Payroll Systems. Yellow copy is retained in agency. Payroll lette
Postal Reports, Stamp Requisitions.

series_title: GAF-82 Request for Transfer of General
series_description: Request for Transfer of General Appropriation and Reallotment of Quarters. Allows state agencies to transfer funds between appropriation line items. O.R.c. 127.13

Includes internal correspondence (letters, memos); also, correspondence from various individuals, companies, and organizations requesting information pertaining to agency and legal intrepreations and other miscellaneous inquiries. This correspondence is
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years, or until audited by the Auditor of State and audit report is released

series_description: Allows state agencies to establish or increase appropriation authority in special funds.
Audit reports and work papers created by an agency's internal audit unit or similar functioning audit.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years after department is audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released

series_description: Postal Records, Registered Mail, Insured Mail Logs and Metered Records.
Publications produces by an agency detailing operations, regulations, and/or procedures of an agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released. Destroy if superseded and of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Form to be filled out by an agency before boxes are transferred to the State Records Center. It is a listing of an agency's holdings, and is maintained by an agency for its record-keeping purposes.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until holdings are destroyed

series_description: Used to request installation or modificattion of any equipment or service provided by telephone company.
Procurement contracts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years after expiration of the contract

series_description: General ledger records.
Organizational Charts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released

series_description: Records documenting the entry and departure of employees and visitors into a State office building during and/or after office hours.
Obligates funds for personal serivce, rental agree ments and capital improvements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years after expiration of the contract

series_description: Statement of need, signed by Purchasing Officer or Fiscal Officer, justifying the purchase of equipment.
Annual financial statements.
Used for claiming reimbursement for in-state and out-of-state travel.
Formal opinions and contract opinions issued to state agencies with all pertinent materials attached thereto.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of adminis- trative or legal value to agency.

series_description: Includes exhibits, movies, and videotape productions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until information is no longer current. Review by State Archives for possible transfer. Destroy if on no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Printed matter published by state agencies for fistribution to the public.
retention_statement: Paper: Upon publication, fulfill the re- quirements of O.R.C 149.11

series_description: Used to order supplies and/or services and may be used to encumber funds for a purchase from another state agency.

Used to make emergency purchases of all materials, supplies, and equipment and to make direct purchases or engage in contracts of services that might include materials that are not professional, technical, or advisory in nature.

All documents relating to expired or inactive license applications and grant or denial of an administrative license or permit. Retain in office for 1 year then transfer all paper documents to a record storage facility for 10 years. After 10 years, shred/destroy documents. Contains all original applications for license or permit, supporting documentation, address, phone numbers and Board correspondence to and from licensee. Also, copies of adjudication orders, consent agreements.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain in office for one year

series_description: Records of all administrative hearings and subsequent board actions/orders are maintained in adjudication journals for public review and inspections, located in the Board office.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in Board office for 10 years

series_description: Consists of complaint forms used to file complaint against licensees and non-licensed persons, along with investigative reports, notes, evidence, and statements. Complaints could be for various violations of the law such as: Misconduct, impairment, unlicensed practice etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 5 years all investigatory documents. If a complaint is unfounded
